title: Review broadcast fan-out for head-of-line blocking
description: Trace how a channel event reaches subscribers and report whether a slow client can stall delivery to others.
body: Use when validating a pub/sub or broadcast path. Actions: trace where one event is delivered to many connections; verify each connection has its own bounded queue and writer so fan-out enqueues without blocking on a slow socket; check for a shared backplane if multiple instances exist; confirm subscriptions are cleaned up on disconnect. Do not accept a synchronous subscriber loop that writes inline. Report the fan-out location and classify it as isolated/bounded or head-of-line-blocking. This skill is read-only against non-production. Validation signals include a slow-client test that does not delay others. Risk boundary: synchronous fan-out plus an unbounded buffer is a stability risk to flag.
